codeReview.summary: Correct, minimal one-file fix for a stack overflow in GlyphCache::populate(). Root cause analysis was thorough and accurate: RasterScratch (~39 KiB with 512 GlyphPoints + 2048 Segments) was placed on the 16 KiB userspace stack via RasterScratch::zeroed(), overflowing into the guard page below USER_STACK_VA (0x7FFF_C000), causing the EC=0x24 Data Abort at FAR=0x7FFFBD60. The fix uses alloc_zeroed + Box::from_raw — the correct pattern to avoid the well-known Rust gotcha where Box::new() constructs on-stack first. The SAFETY comment is accurate: RasterScratch::zeroed() produces all-zero bytes (all fields are numeric types or bool=false), so alloc_zeroed memory is a valid instance. This pattern is already established in the codebase (SvgRasterScratch in compositor) and documented in .factory/library/architecture.md. QEMU visual verification was performed with screenshot confirming text renders correctly.

issues.description: Silent 'return' on allocation failure leaves the glyph cache empty with no diagnostic output. For a ~39 KiB allocation this should never fail in practice, but a debug log or serial print would aid future debugging if the heap is ever exhausted. Low priority since bare-metal logging may not be available at this call site.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/architecture.md line 123 documents SvgPath (~16 KiB) and SvgRasterScratch (~64 KiB) as structs that exceed the 16 KiB userspace stack and must be heap-allocated. RasterScratch (~39 KiB) from the shaping library is now a third such struct but is not listed. Future workers touching this code could reintroduce the stack overflow if they don't know about this constraint.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: architecture.md:123 lists only SVG structs. The fix in drawing/lib.rs:296-313 heap-allocates RasterScratch for the same reason. Adding it to the list would prevent recurrence.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The font-rendering-worker skill procedure mandates TDD (step 2: 'Write Tests First'), but the worker correctly noted that this bare-metal stack overflow cannot be reproduced in host-side tests (host stack is 8 MiB vs 16 KiB on bare metal). The skill doesn't acknowledge environment-specific bugs as a TDD exception. Worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges recommends noting this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill SKILL.md step 2 says 'Write the test, verify it FAILS (red), then implement to make it pass (green)'. Worker handoff skillFeedback: 'some bug fixes are inherently not TDD-able when the bug is environment-specific.' No tests were added, appropriately — QEMU visual verification was the correct validation for this fix.

summary: PASS. The fix correctly heap-allocates RasterScratch (~39 KiB) via alloc_zeroed + Box::from_raw to prevent stack overflow on the 16 KiB userspace stack. Root cause analysis was accurate (FAR=0x7FFFBD60 is below USER_STACK_VA=0x7FFF_C000, confirming guard page hit). The pattern follows established practice (SvgRasterScratch). SAFETY comment is valid. QEMU verification confirmed compositor starts, text renders, no faults. All 1275+ existing tests pass. One non-blocking issue: silent return on allocation failure. Two shared state observations: RasterScratch should be added to architecture.md's large-struct list, and the skill could acknowledge TDD exceptions for environment-specific bugs.
